name: Boeuf a La Bourguignonne Effortless but Sumptuous
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds stew beef, cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and soy sauce in a bowl. Stir in the tomato paste.
2. Place the beef cubes in a very large casserole dish. Pour in the flour mixture, stirring until all the beef is coated.
3. Add the remaining ingredients (onions, carrots, celery, garlic, pepper, salt, thyme, bay leaves, and red wine) to the casserole dish. Stir again to combine.
4. Cover tightly with foil or a lid and place in an oven preheated to 450°F (230°C). Bake for 15 minutes.
5. Reduce the temperature of the oven to 250°F (120°C) and continue cooking until the meat is tender, about 2 to 3 hours. Taste halfway through cooking; add more salt and pepper if needed.
6. Before serving, remove from the oven. Serve with a selection of vegetables such as fresh celery slices, cooked carrots, peas, onions, mushrooms, zucchini, or green beans. These can be added briefly to the stew just before serving, or served alongside on dinner plates.
This dish is best prepared ahead of time and reheated slowly in an oven set to 275°F (135°C), but it also freezes well for future meals.
